what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML page that mounts a composed decision-card view for the rerank-loadout decision by loading layout and decision JSON, rendering the decision-card archetype, wrapping it in an explained slide, and appending it to #mount.
observations: The document title is "Decision — rerank-loadout" and the body contains only a #mount element before scripts.
Inline CSS resets page spacing, applies var(--warm) as the body background, makes #mount at least viewport height, and enables inline-size container behavior.
The page imports Google fonts, shared piece and phone stylesheets, and four runtime scripts.
The script fetches /dna/layouts.json and /content/decisions.json in parallel, then selects the decision whose id is rerank-loadout.
The renderer uses layouts.archetypes["decision-card"], a decision://global/rerank-loadout address, portrait orientation, composed mode, and DNA.decisionDevice(rec).
The resolve hook supplies explanation_source from rec.legibility.why when present and supplies pending for state.
standouts: A fixed decision id drives a generic archetype renderer rather than hardcoded page markup.
The view combines the rendered archetype with DNA.decisionSlide and then marks the result with the dv-c class.
The file-level comment frames this as a composed decision view containing interface and slide-telling in one renderer.
conventions: Uses a small static HTML shell with runtime data loading and rendering delegated to DNA runtime scripts.
Keeps page-specific behavior in an immediately invoked async function.
Uses a single mount node and appends generated DOM instead of authoring the decision markup directly.
Uses container-type on #mount to support container-driven presentation.
concerns: No local error handling for failed fetches, missing decision data, or missing archetype data.
The selected decision record is assumed to exist before being passed into rendering helpers.
External font loading is required for the intended typography but has no local fallback control beyond CSS font-family behavior.
notes: This file is a page wrapper that materializes one pending decision-card slide for the rerank-loadout record through shared DNA renderers.
questions: What should the page show if the rerank-loadout record is absent from /content/decisions.json?
What values besides explanation_source and state can the renderer request through resolve?
What visual or behavioral role does the dv-c class add after rendering?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated composed decision view purpose matches the implementation: it builds a generated decision-card interface and wraps it with slide explanation for a specific decision id.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to instantiate one real decision-card example from shared layout and content sources while keeping the specific page thin and generative.
novelty: A decision URI, decision device helper, archetype renderer, and slide explanation wrapper are combined in a minimal HTML page to produce a composed decision artifact.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete, data-bound page shell for rendering a named decision-card substrate with address identity, pending state, visual device selection, and explained-slide composition.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone HTML page that loads shared layout and decision data, selects rerank-loadout, renders it through the decision-card archetype, wraps it as an explained decision slide, and mounts it into the page.
intention_for_embedding: It was for showing a real composed decision-card instance using shared DNA rendering machinery while preserving the page as a thin data-bound wrapper.
